## Summary
Void weak lensing measures the tangential shear profile around cosmic voids identified in galaxy surveys. Voids produce a characteristic negative convergence signal that probes the matter distribution in underdense regions, complementing cluster lensing and cosmic shear analyses.

## Evidence
DES Year-3 data detected void lensing signal at >10σ significance using ~7000 voids. The void density profiles are well-described by compensated top-hat models. Systematics from void finder algorithms and photo-z uncertainties are being quantified but remain significant sources of uncertainty.

## Significance
Void lensing provides unique sensitivity to modified gravity theories (which predict different void evacuation rates) and to the dark energy equation of state through the growth-geometry degeneracy breaking. It is a relatively new probe that will mature significantly with LSST and Euclid data.
